commit.message:
Fix dead pattern listeners by implementing proper event architecture

Fixes #872

**Problem:**
setupPatternListeners() in scene.js referenced window.officeBackend.onPatternStarted/onPatternEnded which existed in the preload API, but the backend's checkProximityInteractions() didn't emit pattern events. The scene was directly creating patterns and calling visual handlers, bypassing the event system entirely.

**Solution:**
1. Modified office-backend.js checkProximityInteractions() to:
   - Create proper pattern objects with id, type, location, duration
   - Store patterns in this.interactions map
   - Emit 'pattern-started' events that IPC forwards to renderer
   - Auto-end patterns after duration by calling endInteractionPattern()

2. Removed direct pattern creation in scene.js:
   - Deleted ~70 lines of manual PatternSelector calls
   - Removed duplicate awareness indicator suppression logic
   - Now relies on event listeners for pattern rendering

3. Enhanced setupPatternListeners() to:
   - Handle awareness indicator suppression when patterns start
   - Re-enable awareness indicators when patterns end
   - Added #872 reference to console logs

**Architecture:**
Backend emits events → IPC forwards → Preload exposes → Scene listens → Visuals render

This decouples the proximity check (backend concern) from visual rendering (frontend concern) and makes the event flow consistent with other interaction patterns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add file-existence gate before synthesis in debate playbook

Background agents may emit completion notifications before their file
write is flushed to disk. Without an explicit check, the synthesis
agent launches against an incomplete file set and produces a false
'no positions found' result.

Added gate after Round 1 parallel launch and after each Round 2+
parallel launch: verify all expected round-N-<model>.md files exist
(via glob/view) before starting the Orchestrator synthesis step.

Discovered during a live debate run where r1-synthesis found only
context.md despite all 4 Round 1 agents having 'completed'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add missing onPatternStarted/onPatternEnded event listeners to preload.js

The setupPatternListeners() function in scene.js was unable to register
pattern event callbacks because window.officeBackend.onPatternStarted and
window.officeBackend.onPatternEnded were missing from the preload API.

These listeners existed in office-preload.js (lines 260-264) but were not
copied over when the office backend API was integrated into preload.js.

The backend (office-backend.js) already emits 'pattern-started' and
'pattern-ended' events, and the IPC layer (office-ipc.js) already forwards
them as 'office-event:pattern-started' and 'office-event:pattern-ended'.
This change completes the event flow by exposing the listeners to the renderer.

Fixes #870

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix visual overlap: suppress awareness indicators when InteractionVisuals active

When InteractionVisuals patterns trigger for an agent pair, suppress
awareness indicators (thinking bubbles + connection lines) to prevent
visual clutter. Re-enable when pattern ends.

- Add _suppressedAwarenessPairs Set to track active InteractionVisuals
- Clear awareness indicator and add to suppressed set when pattern starts
- Skip suppressed pairs in updateAgentAwareness proximity checks
- Remove from suppressed set when pattern cleanup fires
- Apply to both PatternSelector and fallback quick-huddle paths

Fixes visual overlap reported in #864. Refs #869.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Review: interaction patterns integration — 4 issues filed (#864)

Reviewed interaction-patterns.js, collision-avoidance.js, interaction-visuals.js
and their integration in scene.js + office-backend.js.

Key findings:
- #869 HIGH: Awareness indicators + InteractionVisuals overlap at 72px (root cause of broken visuals)
- #870 MED: moveAgentsToPattern conflicts with active collision avoidance tweens
- #871 LOW: InteractionVisuals double-destroys spawned Phaser objects
- #872 LOW: setupPatternListeners references nonexistent backend method (dead code)

Patterns are well-decoupled individually. Collision avoidance is solid.
Core problem is two uncoordinated visual systems firing at the same range.

Refs #864

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive layout visual regression test suite

- Create tests/layout-fix-visual.spec.js with 26 visual regression tests
- Tests cover interaction alignment at all viewports (1024x768, 1280x720, 1920x1080, 2560x1440)
- Tests verify z-index layering and responsive behavior
- Tests validate design principles (office as main view, no raw output, etc)
- Captures baseline screenshots for future regression detection
- Template based on tests/e2e/ceo-overview-visual.spec.js

Test Results:
- 20/26 tests passing
- 6 tests failing, exposing layout issues:
  * Titlebar buttons misalignment
  * Goal section input misalignment
  * Panel overflow beyond viewport
  * Some buttons/inputs with 0px width

These failures validate the user's report that "half of the screen is broken"
and "interactions are all over the place".

Issue #860

commit.message:
Add IPC handler for agent conversation history (#855)

Implements get-agent-conversations handler to retrieve message history for specific agents.

Changes:
- Added get-agent-conversations IPC handler in meetings-ipc.js
- Handler returns all conversations involving specified agent
- Messages formatted with role (user/agent), content, timestamp
- Conversations sorted by most recent activity
- Exposed handler through preload.js API
- Added comprehensive test suite with performance validation

The handler:
- Returns empty array if no conversation history exists
- Response time < 100ms for 50 messages per conversation
- Supports multiple conversations per agent
- Includes conversation metadata (participants, timestamps, active status)

Fixes #855

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: auto-trigger mentoring sessions for senior+junior pairs (#845)

Add checkMentoringTrigger() to checkInteractionTriggers() in office-backend.js:
- Detects idle senior agent (pm/reviewer/scrum/devops) near idle junior agent
- Triggers startMentoringSession() with ~10% probability per cycle
- Respects 60-minute cooldown per mentor-mentee pair (timeScale-aware)
- Emits 'mentoring-triggered' event for visual rendering

Also triggers mentoring during collaboration phase in office-routines.js:
- 25% chance to replace brainstorm/code-review when pair is senior+junior

The visual rendering (InteractionVisuals.showMentoring with knowledge
transfer particles) was already implemented — this wires up the trigger.

commit.message:
Restore x64 initial user-thread flags for CI smoke test (#37)

The x64 CI smoke test regressed after the initial user thread started
with the wrong RFLAGS value. The kernel reached user-runtime setup but
never entered the PE64 test app in ring3.

- **Root cause**
- `arch/x64/user_runtime.cpp` defined the intended initial flags as
`USER_THREAD_INITIAL_FLAGS = 0x2`, but bootstrap still hardcoded
`0x202`.
- That enabled interrupts before the x64 runtime was ready, which broke
the ring3 handoff exercised by `smoke_x64`.

- **Change**
- Wire the bootstrap thread context to the existing
`USER_THREAD_INITIAL_FLAGS` constant instead of the accidental literal.
- This keeps the initial x64 user thread aligned with the runtime’s
expected startup state.

- **Impact**
- Restores the expected x64 boot path so the PE64 test app can run and
emit the ring3 marker used by CI.
- Removes the drift between the documented/declared startup flags and
the actual bootstrap value.

```cpp
// before
bootstrap.thread_context.flags = 0x202;

// after
bootstrap.thread_context.flags = USER_THREAD_INITIAL_FLAGS;
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mundak <4517164+mundak@users.noreply.github.com>

office backend was destroyed, causing a memory leak.

- Matches cleanup pattern used by activity feed and routines

Refs #842

commit.message:
🔒 Fix critical CORS security vulnerability (#803)

Replace wildcard CORS origin with configurable allowed origins to prevent
CSRF attacks on the office API server.

Changes:
- Replace AllowedOrigins: []string{"*"} with config-based origins
- Fix WebSocket CheckOrigin to validate against allowed origins list
- Add isAllowedOrigin() helper function for origin validation
- Update APIServer constructor to accept allowedOrigins parameter
- Add comprehensive origin validation test suite
- Update documentation to reflect secure implementation

Security Impact:
BEFORE: Any website could make authenticated requests and establish
WebSocket connections, enabling CSRF attacks and data exfiltration.

AFTER: Only explicitly configured origins (via ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var)
can make authenticated cross-origin requests.

Fixes: #803
Filed-by: carol (code review)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace execSync string concat with execFileSync to prevent command injection

Switches all shell commands that accept user input (repo slugs, issue
numbers, branch names, comment bodies) from string-concatenated
execSync() to array-argument execFileSync(). This prevents shell
metacharacter injection (e.g. "; rm -rf /") by bypassing the shell
entirely.

Affected files:
- electron/github-helpers.js: ghCloseIssue, ghCommentIssue,
  ghListOpenIssues, ghCreateIssue
- electron/issue-manager.js: syncIssuesFromGitHub, syncPRAssociations,
  postStakeholderComments, linkPRToIssue, closeIssue
- electron/worktree.js: create (clone, checkout, config), pushBranch

Safe execSync calls with hardcoded strings (git remote get-url, etc.)
are left unchanged.

Fixes #808

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add sync.RWMutex to WebSocketHub to prevent concurrent map panic

The WebSocketHub.clients map was accessed from multiple goroutines
without synchronization:
- Run() goroutine: register/unregister/broadcast (map writes)
- HTTP handlers: GetConnectedClients, GetConnectedAgents (map reads)
- HTTP handlers: BroadcastToAgent (map iterate + delete)

This caused a potential 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic
under load. Fixed by adding sync.RWMutex:
- Write lock in Run() around all map mutations
- Write lock in BroadcastToAgent() for iterate+delete
- Read lock in GetConnectedClients() and GetConnectedAgents()

Also adds a concurrent access test (TestWebSocketHubConcurrentAccess)
to verify the fix under the race detector.

Fixes #811

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix nil pointer dereference in auth claims (#810)

Add nil checks before dereferencing auth claims in API handlers to prevent
panic when JWT auth middleware is not configured.

Fixed handlers:
- moveAgent (line 313): Added agentID extraction with nil check
- sendMessage (line 362-396): Added agentID, agentName, role extraction with nil check
- getSecurityAnalytics (line 533-560): Added agentID, agentName extraction with nil check

When claims is nil (auth not configured), handlers now use 'unknown' as fallback
for audit logging instead of crashing the server.

Note: enterPark, leavePark, and getParkAgents already had proper nil checks.
WebSocket handler gets claims from ValidateJWT which handles errors separately.

Fixes #810

commit.message:
Add OAuth proxy endpoints to strip resource param for Entra compatibility

The MCP SDK (RFC 8707) sends resource=<server-origin> to the authorization
and token endpoints. Entra ID rejects this with AADSTS9010010 when the
resource doesn't match the scope audience (api://client-id).

Fix: Cloudflare Worker now proxies /oauth/authorize and /oauth/token,
stripping the resource parameter before forwarding to Entra. The
authorization_endpoint and token_endpoint in OAuth server metadata now
point to the Worker instead of Entra directly.

- /oauth/authorize: strips resource, 302 redirects to Entra auth endpoint
- /oauth/token: strips resource, proxies POST to Entra token endpoint
- Protected resource metadata resource field stays as origin (satisfies
  SDK's checkResourceAllowed validation)
- All three clients (VS Code, Claude Code, Open Code) now authenticate

Also update docs/11-readonly-mcp-setup.md architecture diagram and add
AADSTS9010010 troubleshooting section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Track tasksCompleted, totalTasks, currentTaskStartTime, velocity, taskHistory
- Keep last 20 tasks in history, calculate velocity from last 60 minutes
- Call startTaskTracking when task begins (both main agents and sub-agents)
- Call completeTaskTracking when task completes
- Added get-agent-metrics IPC handler returning array of agent metrics
- Exposed getAgentMetrics() in preload.js
- Returns: agentName, tasksCompleted, totalTasks, timeOnTaskMs, velocity, status

Fixes #819

commit.message:
fix: add request body size limits to prevent DoS (#826)

Add MaxBodySize middleware (1 MB limit) to both APIServer and
SecureAPIServer routers. Uses http.MaxBytesReader on POST/PUT/PATCH
requests to prevent memory exhaustion from oversized payloads.

All 26 json.NewDecoder(r.Body) call sites across api.go, secure_api.go,
and ceo_overview_handlers.go are now protected by the middleware.

Fixes #826

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix orphan copilot processes on app quit

Add cleanup in before-quit handler to terminate all spawned copilot
processes tracked in ctx.runningProcesses. Without this, child processes
become orphans when the Electron app exits, consuming CPU and memory.

Follow the same pattern as cancel-all handler:
- Terminate process group with SIGTERM (for child processes)
- Terminate the main process
- Clear the tracking maps

Fixes #824

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): fail closed when API_TOKEN not configured (#818)

requireAuth middleware previously called next() when API_TOKEN was unset,
allowing unauthenticated access to all protected endpoints. Now returns
503 Service Unavailable, enforcing secure-by-default behavior.

Also adds a startup warning log when API_TOKEN is missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (#12)

* Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE

Implement the full deposit write workflow, all behind an explicit opt-in:

  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true   (or '1') enables write tools
  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=false  (default) — write tools not registered

## New tools (only registered when write is enabled)

Deposition CRUD:
  create_deposition   POST /api/deposit/depositions
  get_deposition      GET  /api/deposit/depositions/:id
  update_deposition   PUT  /api/deposit/depositions/:id  (metadata JSON)
  delete_deposition   DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id (drafts only)

File management:
  upload_file             PUT to bucket URL; accepts utf-8 or base64 content
  delete_deposition_file  DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id/files/:file_id

Deposition actions:
  publish_deposition   .../actions/publish
  edit_deposition      .../actions/edit  (unlock published record)
  discard_deposition   .../actions/discard
  new_version          .../actions/newversion

## Safety design

- Write tools are not registered at all when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE is false,
  so AI agents never see them unless the operator has opted in.
- Each handler also checks the flag independently (defence-in-depth).
- All write methods call requireAuth() — clear error if key is missing.
- docker-compose.yml exposes ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (default: false).

## Tests (test/zenodo-write.test.ts, 19 tests)

- Gate: write tools absent in tool list when disabled
- Gate: write tools present when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true
- Read-only tools still present when write is enabled
- 10 defence-in-depth handler tests (each write tool returns isError when flag off)
- 2 not-authenticated tests (write enabled, no API key)
- 4 live sandbox round-trip tests (create → upload → update → delete),
  activated only when ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY is set

CI: passes ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY secret and sets ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE
accordingly.

38/38 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: SSRF fix, type safety, encoding validation, upload size limit, description fix

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/zenodo-mcp-server/sessions/0f3481b1-548b-4b98-9fe3-835e10bf6da8

commit.message:
Remove the automated Copilot CI follow-up workflow (#36)

This change removes the workflow that reacted to failed `ci` runs by
opening and assigning a Copilot issue. After this change, failed CI runs
no longer trigger an automated remediation issue.

- **Workflow cleanup**
  - Deletes `.github/workflows/copilot-auto-fix.yml`
  - Stops listening to `workflow_run` completions for the `ci` workflow
- Removes the issue creation path that generated `CI failure on <branch>
(<sha>)` issues

- **Behavioral impact**
  - `ci.yml` remains the only CI workflow in `.github/workflows`
- CI failures are now reported only through the workflow run itself,
with no secondary automation

```yaml
# removed
on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: [ci]
    types: [completed]
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mundak <4517164+mundak@users.noreply.github.com>

Add per-agent DM messaging to Dashboard detail panel

Replace generic 'message the manager' concept with Slack-style DM threads.
Each agent in Dashboard now has a dedicated DM tab where users can send
messages directly to that agent and view conversation history.

Changes:
- Add 'Message' tab to Dashboard detail panel (after Meeting tab)
- Implement loadDMTab() to render per-agent message threads
- Add sendAgentDM() to handle sending messages to specific agents
- Store messages in agentDMs object keyed by agent name
- Add DM thread styles: message bubbles, input box, timestamps
- Connect to backend via window.outworked.replyToAgentDM()
- Listen for agent replies via window.outworked.onAgentDM()

Resolves #782

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
